lines.text: (close) We are going to stay right here with you all night, [pause] because I honestly don't think either one of us has caught our breath since the map turned.
(close) I haven't, no. [pause] They're feeding me pictures faster than I can even keep up, so you'll have to bear with me a little.
(close) Then let's just go where the night takes us. [pause] What's coming in first?
(close) They're telling me the President is walking out to the podium right now, so — [pause] yeah, let's get to that.
(pa) [deliberate] Please welcome the President of the United States.
(pa) Thank you. [pause] Thank you, all of you.
(pa) (then) [deliberate] I do not stand here to celebrate a victory. I stand here to begin a repair.
(pa) [slowly] And to every nation we have wronged, from this room, tonight, we are sorry.
I'll be honest with you, hearing him just say sorry out loud, up there, [pause] that got to me a little.
(close) You and the whole hall. [pause] And I'll tell you who else was watching that, the committee over in Oslo. They've already put his name forward for the Peace Prize.
(close) On the strength of what, though? [pause] The man's been in office a few weeks.
On the agenda he announced, mostly. It's all laid out, so they're just taking him at his word on it, and after a night like tonight, [pause] well.
(close) Now what are we looking at here, [pause] because this one just came in and I genuinely don't know what I'm seeing.
(close) This is the cargo bay of the very first flight out. [pause] Those are pallets of banded hundreds, floor to ceiling, all the way to the back of her.
(close) How much are we talking?
(close) This is the first cash delivery under the Iran Compensation Framework. [pause] First of twelve.
(reading) [flatly] Frontier is proud to serve the goals of this administration.

[deliberate] Mr. Hale, I'm going to be plain with you, same as I've tried to be plain about it from the start. No one man ought to own the only road up off this planet, and right now that's what you've got sitting in your hangar.
(then) The money that put those rockets up there, most of it was public money to start with, [pause] and I think the country's just about done letting one man own the way up and set the price on it.
And we're going to put it back in public hands, and you can call that nationalizing if you want, [pause] because that's just what it is.
(radio) ...and pursuant to the order, the sale, use, or discharge of consumer fireworks is prohibited across all states and territories, effective immediat—
